title: Push operation not compatible with serverless framework
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
Podman authentication failure message differs from docker error message. This sifference causes compatibility issue.

Docker used error message ’no basic auth credentials’ and podman uses ’unauthorized: authentication required’

Project would accept documentation update that explains how to use podman with the framework

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. See https://github.com/serverless/serverless/issues/11120

**Describe the results you received:**
Didn’t test. Just reporting issue reported in another project 😬

**Describe the results you expected:**


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
I’m _quite strongly_ opposed to making error text in CLI a guaranteed-stable API.

For one thing, https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1299 is in flight, which will among other things, shorten error messages to be less repetitive, and it includes that message as well.

---

That said, I don’t know what the Docker Engine API error reporting mechanism does, and that might not necessarily use exactly the same text as the CLI. At least looking at https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/v1.41/#section/Errors , there seems to be no stable API mechanism either.
I agree serverless should check both messages.

I agree that it might not be the best idea to check error from error message. Error code would be a far better alternative.

Unfortunatley docker api provides none. Considering podman is emulating docker api, it would be ideal returned errors were same. If error message is checked by one application, who knows how many do the same. Better compatibility improves adoption

If I read original issue right, it was about using podman as daemon (via socket) And if I remember correctly, podman socket was divided to docker compatible api & podman api by request path. Error message could be changed only in docker compatible api. This wouldn’t cause problems with existing api clients as they are expecting to be communicating with docker

Podman team has done awesome job with api compatibility. I guess the question here is about where to draw the line. Is it enough to cover happy case scenarios or should major fail paths also be included.

The documentation part is also something you could assist serverless team. Would I not write so many typos, I might do it myself 😂

If this is some kind of command that interactively wraps the API and restarts on failures, I think the _ultimately right_ thing to do here is to have a specific “validate credentials” call that returns relevant values. Trying to push, doing all kinds of preparatory work, and then failing, and having to restart that, seems wasteful.

That would be an entirely new feature, probably in a Podman-specific API.

---

Looking at the Docker API compatibility aspect: Docker does have _a_ way to determine if the error is lack of credentials: https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/924edb948c2731df3b77697a8fcc85da3f6eef57/client/image_push.go#L39 , and that’s ultimately decided by checking the HTTP status code: https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/924edb948c2731df3b77697a8fcc85da3f6eef57/errdefs/http_helpers.go#L21 , _not_ the error text.

Now, the 401 value is not actually documented in https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/v1.41/#tag/Image/operation/ImagePush , but looking at the other errors, it does seem that the HTTP statuses is what API callers are supposed to be using.

---

But, uh, does the API matter at all here? A simple search for that string ends up at https://github.com/serverless/serverless/blob/bb37f4fe75ff5234fae48ada433cd52ddf51cb91/lib/plugins/aws/provider.js#L1977-L1987, which seems like invoking the Docker CLI binary (or `podman-docker` I suppose), and substring matching on the CLI output.

My purely personal opinion is that I’m not really interested in that kind of compatibility, but I can’t speak for the Podman project.
@skorhone, can you work with the provided information?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we have never heard any feedback, I am closing.  Reopen if you want to continue working on this.
